title: Grilled Chicken Rice Bowl

subs.name: 500 g fish fillets — cook 3-4 min per side on the tawa
1 cup quinoa — cook with 1.75 cups water for extra protein
gingelly oil for a nuttier south-Indian aroma

steps.text: Combine garlic, ginger, lemon juice, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, garam masala, black pepper, half the oil and salt in a bowl. Score the chicken with diagonal cuts 1 cm deep, then rub the marinade all over. Let it rest 10 minutes at room temperature.

Wash the rice until the water runs clear. Cook with 1.75 cups water and a pinch of salt — 2 whistles in a pressure cooker, or simmer covered in a saucepan for 12-14 minutes. Fluff with a fork and keep warm.
Heat remaining oil in a tawa or frying pan on high flame. Place the chicken in a single layer and grill without moving for 4-5 minutes until firm charred marks appear on the underside.
Flip and cook the other side 4-5 minutes until the juices run clear when the thickest part is pierced. Remove from heat and rest 3 minutes before slicing into strips.
Divide rice between two bowls, top with sliced chicken, scatter spring onion and coriander leaves over the top, and finish with a fresh squeeze of lemon. Serve immediately.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 500 g fish fillets — cook 3-4 min per side on the tawa (pescatarian swap); rice → 1 cup quinoa — cook with 1.75 cups water for extra protein; oil → gingelly oil for a nuttier south-Indian aroma
